title: Enterprises power agentic workflows in Cloudflare Agent Cloud with OpenAI
description: Cloudflare brings OpenAI’s GPT-5.4 and Codex to Agent Cloud, enabling enterprises to build, deploy, and scale AI agents for real-world tasks with speed and security.

title: ChatGPT for operations teams
description: Learn how operations teams use ChatGPT to streamline workflows, improve coordination, standardize processes, and drive faster execution.

title: Applications of AI at OpenAI
description: Explore how OpenAI products like ChatGPT, Codex, and APIs bring AI into real-world use for work, development, and everyday tasks.

title: Brainstorming with ChatGPT
description: Learn how to use ChatGPT to brainstorm ideas, organize thinking, and turn rough concepts into structured, actionable plans.

title: ChatGPT for marketing teams
description: Learn how marketing teams use ChatGPT to plan campaigns, generate content, analyze performance, and move from ideas to execution faster.

title: SPPO: Sequence-Level PPO for Long-Horizon Reasoning Tasks
description:
arXiv:2604.08865v1 Announce Type: new

title: Enhancing LLM Problem Solving via Tutor-Student Multi-Agent Interaction
description:
arXiv:2604.08931v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Human cognitive development is shaped not only by individual effort but by structured social interaction, where role-based exchanges such as those between a tutor and a learner, enable solutions that neither could achieve alone. Inspired by these deve

title: PilotBench: A Benchmark for General Aviation Agents with Safety Constraints
description:
arXiv:2604.08987v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: As Large Language Models (LLMs) advance toward embodied AI agents operating in physical environments, a fundamental question emerges: can models trained on text corpora reliably reason about complex physics while adhering to safety constraints? We add

title: SEA-Eval: A Benchmark for Evaluating Self-Evolving Agents Beyond Episodic Assessment
description:
arXiv:2604.08988v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Current LLM-based agents demonstrate strong performance in episodic task execution but remain constrained by static toolsets and episodic amnesia, failing to accumulate experience or optimize strategies across task boundaries. While the Self-Evolving

title: SAGE: A Service Agent Graph-guided Evaluation Benchmark
description:
